review_title: Manage Engine is superior to NinjaOne
description: Very professional, Very supportive, and made onboarding our devices super easy. We were coming from NinjaOne after a horrible issue with them and their billing. ManageEngine was able to make it so simple we moved operations quickly and without major impact to our users. 100% ManageEngine > NinjaOne. The value you get also puts things like Altera where you pay a massive fee per tech for "unlimited" endpoints. Except its not unlimited; its limited to how many 1 tech can do...
author: Jamie M.
pros: The inclusiveness of the product, the segementation between Device management and mobile device is a long running unnecessary division. The product has a natural and easy to use flow that can be picked up quickly by a Helpdesk I to a Sr. Engineer
cons: There is not a ton I dont like, #1 issue the drop down from changing clients sometimes is a bit sticky and takes a second selection. A few more wizards would also be nice.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Robust Endpoint Management for Serious IT Operations
description: I run an MSP and use Endpoint Central to manage multiple mid-size clients. It’s not just another tool — it’s a control tower. After implementation, we cut troubleshooting time in half and gained real visibility over patch compliance and endpoint health. It takes effort to learn, but once properly deployed, it gives you scale, traceability, and peace of mind. It’s ideal for teams that manage infrastructure seriously and need to show compliance and results, not just "monitoring with pretty graphs."
author: Daniel B.
pros: Patch management that works. Reliable, flexible scheduling, and solid reporting. Built-in compliance reports aligned with CIS Controls help justify decisions to leadership. Easy-to-use asset inventory with exportable, filtered reports for hardware and software. Complete vulnerability management, with CVE correlation and direct remediation actions. Replaces multiple tools by centralizing patching, inventory, scripting, compliance, and more.
cons: The platform is very extensive, which means a real learning curve. It’s not plug-and-play for junior techs. The interface feels dated in some areas, and reporting dashboards require manual setup.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Using manage engine endpoint central msp has some room for improvement
author: hasina B.
pros: It is a smooth running system where you can find all your machines and use it to patch,push software and update etc.
cons: The main issue i feel is the machine sometimes is shown offline even though it is online and we need to restart the machine to enable it.

review_title: MDM the best way to protect our Mobile enviroment
description: It's a great tool; I would recommend it to many clients and anyone who needs to manage their devices, given the wide range of devices it supports.
author: gustavo D.
pros: the use of the remote troubleshoting tool, it is easy to use, and it help use to have a faster helpdesk than others tools
cons: The way the settings are arranged is a bit messy; it needs to be more organized in terms of where each setting should go.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I like Endpoint Central MSP, it's essential for a good control of your company and customers. And it offer rebranding.
author: Jose Luis R.
pros: I have used on premise and cloud version and it is very good solution for control and monitorice your company and customers.
cons: You pay for users, for extra functioanlity, for multilanguage. I would like to learning portal and more integration with a different ManageEngine applications.

description: Reliable for managing multiple clients' devices with good support; a few usability improvements would make it excellent. Great amount of features for a price.
author: Omar A.
pros: Comprehensive device management, strong automated patching features and a centralised dashboard, good integration features with other ManageEngine core products such as Service Desk Plus MSP.
cons: Some limitations for smaller customers, sometimes errors encountered aren't clear but support always helpful.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Perfect for Client Management and extensive management options
description: Very good support, if problems should pop up. Overall great experience
author: Yannick G.
pros: Quick and easy management, simple onboarding and efficient management. PowerShell and EventViewer access without remoting into the machines.
cons: MSP Version doesn't support deploying configurations to multiple customers at the same time. Update schedules need to be created on a customer to customer basis

review_title: My experience with ManageEngine Endpoint Central MSP
description: Love the support and that I can always get dedicated service whenever I need it. From actual people, thats the most important part.
author: Donny H.
pros: The central hub of management is the best part. I dont have to jump around to various different servers and software pieces to achieve what I can achieve in one place. Love the dashboards and simplicity of being able to visually see what I'm looking for without having to investigate every time.
cons: The cloud version has its benefits but not sure if they out weigh the benefits of the on-prem solution. Seems like the cloud version doesn't offer as much.

description: Overall, my experience has been very positive. It’s a great product for IT teams looking to automate patching, streamline software deployment, and provide reliable remote access. It simplifies daily operations and enhances productivity across the board.
author: Wilbert H.
pros: ManageEngine Endpoint Central MSP is very easy to use and straightforward to set up, even for teams that may not have a lot of time to dedicate to deployment. The interface is intuitive, and its remote access capabilities work seamlessly, allowing technicians to quickly and reliably assist users. The platform’s patch management and software deployment features are among its strongest advantages—both are automated, efficient, and help significantly reduce manual workload. These capabilities make it an excellent tool for maintaining a secure, well‑managed environment across multiple endpoints. Overall, its combination of usability and powerful automation features makes it a great fit for MSPs and IT departments.
cons: Support could be improved. While the product itself is strong, responses to support tickets could be faster and provide clearer guidance.

description: Overall, our experience with ManageEngine Endpoint Central MSP has been very positive. The platform provides a comprehensive set of tools that allow us to efficiently manage endpoints across multiple clients from a single interface. Features such as patch management, remote support, and automation significantly reduce administrative effort and improve service quality. Once configured properly, the system runs reliably and helps maintain a secure and well-managed endpoint environment. It has become an important part of our daily operations and enables us to provide consistent and efficient IT management for our customers.
author: Luca L.
pros: Excellent multi-tenant management, making it easy to manage multiple customer environments from a single console. Strong patch management for both operating systems and third-party applications, which helps maintain security and compliance. Remote troubleshooting tools are reliable and make it easy to support users quickly. Automation and policy-based management reduce manual work and help standardize configurations across clients. Software deployment and inventory management features provide good visibility and control over managed endpoints.
cons: The user interface can feel a bit complex at first, especially for new administrators. Some advanced configurations and reports require time to learn due to the large number of available options. Initial setup and policy configuration can take some time to fully optimize for MSP workflows.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Endpoint Central can fit your company's needs.
description: The experience overall is very good. It makes managing a medium organization and all its products a lot easier.
author: Devan S.
pros: The ability to manage and control our full windows/linux environment. Very nice for managing a large number of different devices. The automated patch management feature saves the team time and keeps everything up to date and secure.
cons: The costs associated with each module can be a bit pricey.

description: Great timely support, fast web chat options
author: Brent S.
pros: Fast and efficient support and solutions.
cons: Nothing, maybe just keep developing software.

review_title: Nice Product to manage End Point devices
description: Nice Product to manage End Point devices
author: Sampath V.
pros: Inventory , Custiomized Reports and Patch management overview
cons: Hardware inventory and Remote access and mobile device enrollment

review_title: Single products to manage your data, help to team for remote desktop
author: Nipun Y.
pros: It help the team to easily take a remote the desktop amd also reduce the time for patch updating on all the user
cons: Their support is low right now for my organization

author: Harsh S.
pros: It makes patching activity very easy, As there are some change also which are helping us in patching activity very much.
cons: It does not have any option for creating graphical custom reports

description: Overall, the amount of time I've saved remoting into machines, automatically pushing software using configurations when making a new PC, pushing registry changes, checking things with the remote Command Prompt, remotely patching and seeing remote computer performance has really given me a ton of my time back. That is absolutely crucial for us since I'm the only IT person we have for a company with over 100 computers to track.
author: Chris A.
pros: The automated patching. This has done wonders for freeing up time I could spend doing more important things like furthering our company in software development.
cons: I think how hard it is to uninstall programs. It doesn't get a very complete list from individual users. For example, I had zoom installed for several people and it was giving me problems in Tenable.io. I couldn't use this software because it couldn't find it. I had to uninstall each one manually.

description: Overall positive experience and decent value for money. I would recommend this for most mid-size organizations looking for a decent option at a reasonable price.
author: Carleton M.
pros: The suite of features available is very large and useful. The sky is the limit when it comes to what you want to manage and report on for any managed devices. During the pandemic report management become of the utmost important as well as inventorying everyone computer. This product was able to allow us to do it seamlessly and effectively.
cons: No major complaints, but the UI could be better. It could be cleaner and more logically laid out but the have been making improvements over the last few years. The other thing is that support is inconsistent. It really depends upon who you get and when seems to dictate the quality of service that you get.

description: this resolved lots of issues in terms of patch management as devices were not patched in time and this includes 3rd party apps and does not focus on one app or solution. inventory assist with asset management.
author: Ali M.
pros: Patch management includes auto deployment to ensure devices are updated frequently and are compliant.
cons: Unable to modify Secure Gateway Server URL from Admin portal, this can only be done by changes in the config file.

author: Michel M.
pros: easy to use and very well tought of. I loved it since day one and used other products.
cons: not available to install on docker or linux

description: Best remote tool I have ever used.
author: Shane B.
pros: Ease of use, overall dependability, nice tool set.
cons: Can have glitches/bugs occasionally. Do occasionally get lag in screen displays and the occasional disconnect.

description: Overall is really powerfull, full of features and really easy to use.
author: Jose C.
pros: I really like the remote features like sending CMD commands remotely.
cons: Sometimes it might get stuck on logins, or the session times-out and there is no way of extending the timeout.

author: Madhumitha C.
pros: Easy to Configure, Support team response is good, Easy to use.
cons: While taking remote frequency disconnects happen.

author: Martin B.
pros: Having everything available in one space
cons: Having adds pop up at random, would prefer mail marketing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Good product as having too much features
author: Ajay K.
pros: Dashboard and features, easy to use and understanding about administration
cons: Software mangement and MS patch mangement

review_title: Simple easy to use & value for money
author: Michael C.
pros: Easy & simple to remote monitoring & management in my current operation environment to fix issues & also value for $$$
cons: Local management support would be great.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial
